In every yama the tatvas rise - this is only a concept; a
concept to comprehend the rhythm of day and night. Similar
to Ayurveda which uses the concept ofVata-Pitta-Kapha (Air
Bile-Phelm) to comprehend the physical and mental diseases
aimed at treating and curing them, astrology tries to study
the rhythm of time using hypothetical constructs. Following
the same line of thinking here astrology is trying to
comprehend the rhythm of 'Ahoratra' and its subdivision
Yama using Pancha tatva construct. Such constructs or
concepts gain acceptance when they prove useful, in practical
application and match with observed results. Hypothetical
constructs used in all holistic subjects gained acceptence only
because they proved useful in application, passing the test of
time, providing logical derivations that matched with observed
results. This is true for most of the ancient indian theories
